rankings.title: How SpaceX Works
summary_bullets: SpaceX's cost strategy: first-principles material analysis
Vertical integration captures savings from cheap atoms
Standardization enables high-volume manufacturing flywheel
Rapid iteration treats failures as data
Culture of questioning requirements and visible failure
rankings.display_title: SpaceX’s Success System: First-Principles Cost, Vertical Integration, and Rapid Iteration

rankings.title: Joseph Pulitzer: The Inventor of Mass Media
summary_bullets: Pulitzer rose from homeless immigrant to media mogul.
He pioneered mass-circulation journalism with sensationalism.
His blindness and isolation marked his later years.
He used aggressive promotion and cost control.
His life story is both inspiring and cautionary.

rankings.title: How Honda Works
summary_bullets: Biography of Soichiro Honda’s life and philosophy
Honda’s obsession with engines and improvement
Partnership with Fujisawa for business side
Super Cub became best-selling motor vehicle
Racing used as R&D to drive innovation

rankings.title: Tortured Into Greatness: The Life of Andre Agassi
summary_bullets: Andre Agassi hated tennis despite being #1.
Father tortured him into greatness with relentless training.
Agassi’s mental struggles and self-sabotage are central.
He rebuilt his career from rank 141 after drug use.
Key lesson: helping others gives more meaning than winning.
rankings.display_title: Tortured Into Greatness: The Life of Andre Agassi

rankings.title: Steve Jobs in Exile
summary_bullets: Steve Jobs’ 12-year exile at NeXT
Book chronicles his mistakes and transformation
NeXT nearly bankrupt multiple times
Jobs learned to lead and sell software
Acquisition by Apple in 1997

rankings.title: How Arnold Schwarzenegger Won
summary_bullets: Arnold discovered bodybuilding at age 15
He idolized Reg Park and studied him obsessively
Trained twice daily, even in freezing conditions
Used positive affirmations and visualization daily
Applied bodybuilding discipline to acting and business

rankings.title: How Roger Federer Works.
summary_bullets: Federer won only 54% of points.
Mental discipline was his biggest weakness.
He built a team for long-term health.
Life outside tennis prevented burnout.
He learned to control inner rage.

rankings.title: The Creative Genius of Rick Rubin
summary_bullets: Host summarizes Rick Rubin's book on creativity.
Key themes: habits, intuition, patience, self-doubt.
Rubin's approach: treat art as a way of being.
Examples from John Wooden, Napoleon, Steve Jobs.
Emphasis on submerging in great works.
rankings.display_title: Rick Rubin's 'The Creative Act' – Book Summary and Insights on Creativity

rankings.title: Phil Knight: Founder of Nike
summary_bullets: Phil Knight's early life and inspiration
Founding Nike from a crazy idea
Bill Bowerman as mentor and co-founder
Financial struggles and relentless growth
Going public and personal regrets

rankings.title: How Rick Rubin Works
summary_bullets: Minimalist "production by reduction" philosophy.
Left Def Jam to preserve creative vision.
Revived Johnny Cash's late career.
Prefers preparation over studio time.
Works only with self-motivated artists.

rankings.title: The Relentless Missionary Creating AGI: Demis Hassabis
summary_bullets: Chess prodigy founded DeepMind for AGI.
Sold to Google to avoid fundraising distractions.
AlphaGo defeated world champion at Go.
AlphaFold solved protein folding, won Nobel.
Demis is a missionary, not mercenary.

rankings.title: How Elon Thinks
summary_bullets: Book summary of Elon Musk’s ideas
First principles thinking explained with examples
Musk’s “algorithm” for engineering (5 steps)
Emphasis on manufacturing and making stuff
Includes sponsor ads for Ramp and Vanta

rankings.title: Excellent Advice For Living: 79 Maxims from a Wise Old Man
summary_bullets: Host reads and comments on Kevin Kelly’s life maxims
Maxims cover kindness, habits, deadlines, and perspective
Host adds personal anecdotes and quotes from famous thinkers
rankings.display_title: Kevin Kelly’s Life Maxims Reviewed with Personal Anecdotes

rankings.title: The Simple Genius of Jony Ive
summary_bullets: Jony Ive’s design philosophy: simplify, remove, reduce.
His father nurtured his early design curiosity.
Partnership with Steve Jobs reversed Apple’s engineering-led culture.
iMac handle made technology approachable.
iPod and iPhone built on obsessive attention to detail.

rankings.title: How To Run Down A Dream
summary_bullets: Sam Hinkie’s journey from analyst to NBA GM
Bill Gurley’s five guidelines for dream careers
Examples: Bobby Knight, Bob Dylan, Danny Meyer
Emphasis on passion, study, mentors, peers, generosity
Host’s personal connection to the talk and book
rankings.display_title: Bill Gurley’s Five Guidelines for Pursuing a Dream Career (with Sam Hinkie Story)
